title: Adopt Server-Backed Agent Runtimes
description: Adopt server-backed per-task execution for supported coding agents. Make codex use codex app-server and opencode use opencode serve by default, introduce a shared task-session runtime above backend-specific protocols, preserve explicit legacy fallbacks during migration, and capture the architecture in an ADR.

title: Write ADR-002 server-backed agent runtimes
description: Document the decision to move default codex execution to codex app-server and default opencode execution to opencode serve, using a shared task-session runtime above protocol-specific drivers while preserving explicit legacy fallbacks during migration.
close_reason: ADR created at docs/adr/ADR-002-server-backed-agent-runtimes.md

title: Adopt Codex app-server as default backend
description: Implement a codex app-server driver over stdio JSON-RPC, use one runner-owned ephemeral thread and turn per task, normalize Codex events into runner progress, and keep codex CLI available as an explicit fallback backend.
close_reason: Codex app-server epic landed on main

title: Implement Codex app-server stdio JSON-RPC driver
description: Start codex app-server per task over stdio, perform initialize and initialized handshake, create one runner-owned ephemeral thread and turn, send the task prompt, and stop the process when the task ends.
close_reason: Superseded by narrower Codex app-server implementation slices for one-shot execution

title: Map Codex notifications and completion into runner progress
description: Translate Codex app-server thread, turn, item, tool, and completion notifications into the shared normalized event model and runner progress updates, including review-mode completion handling.
close_reason: Landed on main

title: Integrate codex backend selection and preserve codex-cli fallback
description: Wire yolo-agent and related entrypoints so the default codex backend resolves to the app-server driver while codex-cli remains available as an explicit legacy fallback backend.
close_reason: Landed on main

title: Add tests for the Codex app-server runner
description: Add unit and integration coverage for Codex app-server startup, handshake, event streaming, cancellation, teardown, and fallback selection using the shared test harnesses.
close_reason: Landed on main

title: Start codex app-server process and complete initialize handshake
description: Start codex app-server per task over stdio, establish the JSON-RPC transport, perform initialize and initialized handshake, and return a typed session handle without yet sending task turns.
close_reason: Landed on main

title: Execute one ephemeral Codex thread and turn
description: Using the initialized Codex app-server session, create one runner-owned ephemeral thread, start one turn with the task prompt, and capture the final turn-completion result without full event normalization yet.
close_reason: Landed on main

title: Add Codex app-server cancellation and teardown controls
description: Add interrupt, cancellation, graceful shutdown, and forced teardown behavior for the per-task codex app-server process and ephemeral session so runner-owned cleanup is reliable.
close_reason: Landed on main

title: Converge ACP on shared runtime
description: Refactor ACP execution to reuse the shared task-session runtime so ACP inherits the same lifecycle supervision, event normalization, approval handling, timeout behavior, logging, and teardown semantics as the new server-backed backends.

title: Refactor ACP runner onto shared task-session lifecycle
description: Move ACP execution onto the shared runtime so ACP startup, cancellation, timeout handling, teardown, and artifact collection behave the same way as the new server-backed backends.
close_reason: Superseded by smaller ACP convergence slices

title: Build shared task-session runtime
description: Introduce a shared runtime layer that owns per-task process lifecycle, readiness checks, execution control, normalized event streaming, approval/question handling, cancellation, teardown, logging, and artifacts for server-backed and protocol-backed agents.
notes: triage_reason=review rejected: Wire `internal/tasksession` into the actual server-backed and protocol-backed runner/adaptor paths so it owns lifecycle/readiness/execution/event/log/artifact behavior end-to-end, add the missing `cancelling` lifecycle transition, and get the full test suite back to green before re-requesting review.
close_reason: All children completed

title: Define common task-session runtime interfaces and event model
description: Define the shared runtime contracts for process lifecycle, execution control, normalized progress events, approvals, questions, cancellation, teardown, logging, and artifacts so Codex app-server, OpenCode serve, and ACP can plug into one execution framework.

title: Implement managed child-process supervisor and teardown policy
description: Implement the process supervisor that starts one backend process per task, waits for readiness, applies timeouts, performs graceful shutdown, escalates to forced kill when needed, and guarantees cleanup on completion, failure, or cancellation.
notes: triage_reason=review rejected: `go test ./internal/codingagents ./cmd/yolo-agent ./cmd/yolo-linear-worker` does not pass on this branch: `cmd/yolo-agent` fails `TestE2E_YoloAgentRunCompletesSeededTKTask`, `TestE2E_CodexTKConcurrency2LandsViaMergeQueue`, and `TestE2E_ClaudeConflictRetryPathFinalizesWithLandingOrBlockedTriage/lands_after_one_retry`, with tasks ending in an empty status instead of `closed`; until those regressions are resolved, the supervisor/teardown implementation does not satisfy the required acceptance criteria and tests.
close_reason: Recovered supervisor implementation on main, removed generated beads artifacts, and validated with go test ./internal/codingagents ./internal/tk ./cmd/yolo-agent ./cmd/yolo-linear-worker plus go build ./...

title: Implement shared readiness, cancellation, and logging helpers
description: Add reusable helpers for stdio and HTTP readiness checks, cancellation propagation, artifact assembly, normalized event emission, and backend stderr or protocol trace logging.
notes: triage_reason=review rejected: Blocking: [runtime_helpers.go](/Users/egv/dev/yolo-runner/.yolo-runner/clones/yolo-c72.4.3/internal/contracts/runtime_helpers.go#L103) makes `BackendLogSidecarPath("")` return `.stderr.log` / `.protocol.log` instead of no sidecar path, so reusable callers with an unset log path can create/read stray files in the cwd; add an empty-input guard and a regression test for the empty-path case. Also, this cannot be marked pass while `go test ./...` is red in this workspace (`internal/tk` has undefined `parseTicketQuery`, and `cmd/yolo-agent` has failing e2e tests), even though the directly affected package tests currently pass.

title: Add protocol test harnesses for stdio JSON-RPC and HTTP/SSE
description: Create reusable fake protocol harnesses so runtime and backend tests can simulate Codex stdio JSON-RPC flows, OpenCode HTTP and SSE flows, and future ACP convergence without requiring real agent binaries.
notes: triage_reason=review rejected: Fix `FakeHTTPSSEHarness.SendSSE` so it never holds the global mutex while performing potentially blocking sends; snapshot the client list under lock, release it before delivery, and make slow/disconnected clients unable to deadlock bursty SSE test flows.

title: Adopt OpenCode serve as default backend

title: stdinProcess interface + osStdinProcess struct
description: Define stdinProcess interface (Stdin, Stdout, Wait, Stop, Kill) and osStdinProcess struct. No method bodies. Compile conformance assertion only.
close_reason: stdinProcess interface and osStdinProcess struct defined; conformance test green

title: Execute: emit assistant output events to EventSink
description: Parse type=assistant messages; call EventSink.HandleEvent() with TaskSessionEventTypeOutput. No-op if EventSink nil.
close_reason: done

title: Execute: tool_use approval (detect + respond + emit)
description: Parse type=tool_use; call ApprovalHandler if non-nil (default approve); write y\n to stdin; emit TaskSessionEventTypeApprovalRequired event.
close_reason: done

title: Cancel (SIGINT) and Teardown (graceful + force)
description: Cancel(force=false)->Stop(). Teardown: close stdin, Stop, wait with stopTimeout, escalate to Kill. closeOnce guards double-close. closeLogs() closes log files.
close_reason: done

title: Log path, log files, interface conformance
description: resolveStdinLogPath() -> runner-logs/claude/<taskID>.jsonl. openStdinLogFiles() creates stdout+stderr sidecar via contracts.BackendLogSidecarPath. var _ contracts.TaskSessionRuntime = (*TaskSessionRuntime)(nil). make fmt && make test && make lint all green.
close_reason: done

title: osStdinProcess.Wait(), Stdin(), Stdout()
description: Implement Wait() with sync.Once, close(waitDone), store waitErr. Add Stdin()/Stdout() accessors.
close_reason: Wait() idempotent; Stdin()/Stdout() accessors implemented

title: osStdinProcess.Stop() and Kill()
description: Stop() sends os.Interrupt; Kill() sends SIGKILL. Both tolerate os.ErrProcessDone.
close_reason: Stop() sends SIGINT; Kill() sends SIGKILL; both tolerate ErrProcessDone

title: startStdinProcess function
description: StdinProcessSpec struct + startStdinProcess: exec.Command with piped Stdin/Stdout, Stderr to writer, cmd.Start().

title: TaskSessionRuntime, NewTaskSessionRuntime, Start()
description: stdinProcessStarter interface + func adapter. TaskSessionRuntime struct. NewTaskSessionRuntime(binary, args). StdinTaskSession skeleton. Start() opens log files, spawns process, returns session with waitDone goroutine.
close_reason: TaskSessionRuntime, NewTaskSessionRuntime, Start(), StdinTaskSession skeleton all implemented; conformance var passes

title: WaitReady: detect system/init event
description: WaitReady() reads JSONL from stdout goroutine via sync.Once; returns nil when {type:system,subtype:init} received. Respects readyTimeout.
close_reason: WaitReady scans stdout for system/init event; returns nil on receipt

title: WaitReady: early exit and timeout errors
description: Return 'exited before ready' error when process exits without init event. Return 'timed out' + stderr summary when readyTimeout exceeded.
close_reason: WaitReady returns 'exited before ready' and 'timed out' errors correctly

title: Execute: write prompt to stdin
description: Execute() calls WaitReady, then writes prompt+newline to proc.Stdin(). Return error if write fails.
close_reason: done

title: Execute: result event detection (success + error)
description: Parse type=result/subtype=success -> nil. Parse type=result/subtype=error_during_execution -> error. Process exit without result -> error.
close_reason: done
